page: Microsoft Azure DC48ads_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DCadsv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 48 dedicated vCPUs. The system is virtualized using the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

The server features 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. Local storage is bundled with the instance, providing 1843 GB of local disk capacity. Additionally, the instance is capable of utilizing Premium Storage for external disk attachments.

This configuration does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and substantial local storage capacity offer a balanced resource density for workloads requiring consistent compute resources and local scratch space without the overhead of GPU accelerators.

Given the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the integrated local storage, this server is configured for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, enterprise applications, and development environments requiring dedicated CPU execution.
description: Microsoft Azure DC48ads_v6 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine from the DCadsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 1843 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage configurations, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. With its dedicated CPU allocation and substantial local disk capacity, this server provides a stable environment for resource-dense applications. It is suited for database hosting, enterprise applications, and general-purpose workloads requiring consistent compute performance and local scratch space.
